summary: The complete legal domain has at most fifteen ternary digits, so digit extraction has a fixed maximum workload that cannot support an honest asymptotic scaling verdict.

workload_bound.source_constraint: n is an integer from 1 through 10000000

replacement_checks.evidence: Each loop iteration removes one ternary digit, and every legal input is smaller than 3^15, so the accepted source executes at most fifteen iterations.
Authored cases cover the minimum and maximum inputs, low and higher digit-two failures, a single higher power, sparse valid sums, and the dense fifteen-digit valid sum.
